# vnfin

> Clean-room, no-key, open-source Python library for Vietnam financial-market data: equity prices,
> fundamentals, mutual funds, indices, gold, FX, crypto, and macro indicators. Every domain has
> multi-source failover and typed results with explicit units. No API key is needed for the default
> path of any domain.

Install: `pip install git+https://github.com/hungson175/vnfin.git` (add `[pandas]` for `.to_dataframe()`). Python ≥ 3.10.

Five rules that apply everywhere: (1) no key needed (only optional BYOK: `FRED_API_KEY`, `VNFIN_BTMC_WIDGET_KEY`); (2) `client()` = failover chain, `source()` = single primary — except `gold`, which has no `client()` (use `vn()`/`world()`); (3) units are explicit on the typed result, read them (equities VND, indices "points", gold VND/lượng or USD/oz, fundamentals raw VND, FX "VND per 1 base"); (4) `start`/`end` are required for history and validated before any network call; (5) `.to_dataframe()` needs the optional `pandas` extra.

## Docs

- [AI usage guide](docs/ai-usage.md): comprehensive, copy-paste, progressive-disclosure reference for AI agents — start here. Covers all 8 domains with verified examples, units, and gotchas.
- [README](README.md): human getting-started, domains & sources table, install.
- [Public API & naming standard](docs/api.md): the `client()`/`source()` facade and per-domain entry points.
- [Units & currencies](docs/units.md): canonical unit per domain — read when a number looks off.
- [Stability & versioning policy](docs/stability.md): SemVer guarantees, the public-API snapshot test, and the opt-in upstream health harness.

## Agent skill

- [vnfin skill](skills/vnfin/SKILL.md): an Anthropic-style skill that teaches an AI to use this library; includes a per-domain reference.

## Sources & provenance

- [docs/sources/](docs/sources/): per-source endpoints, terms, rate limits, and clean-room provenance for every adapter.

## Optional

- [docs/design/](docs/design/): design notes (failover/redundancy, FX sources, macro no-key + BYOK).
- [CHANGELOG](CHANGELOG.md): release notes.
